title: Monkey Memory Game
description: I'm currently working on a fun project inspired by my visit to the Indianapolis Zoo this past summer. For this project, I'm using Vite with React for the frontend and Firebase for the backend and database. The game will have a login feature, and I'm planning to use Chart.js to show each user's average score over time, so players can track their progress.

title: Fictional University
description: Creating a custom Wordpress Template for a fictional university. Taking my first steps in relearning PHP and familiarizing myself with Wordpress. I am following a UDemy Course called 'How to create a custom Wordpress Template's.

title: Profolio Website 2025
description: Rebuilding my portfolio website in 2025! I started by brainstorming designs in Figma, mapping out how I want the site to look and feel—you can check out my ideas below. I'm also relearning Prepros for SCSS compilation and diving back into React. It feels great to take these first steps toward personal growth and get inspired to work on my own projects again.

title: SkyAlert - Weather App
description: I built a weather forecast app using the OpenWeatherMap API. It can detect a user’s location to provide a local forecast, and users can also search for the weather in any city. The app includes features like switching between Celsius and Fahrenheit, viewing a 3-day forecast, and checking the UV Index, wind speed, humidity, and more. I plan to add additional features in the future to make it even more interactive and useful.

title: Profolio Website 2023
description: My old profolio site hosted on gitHub.

title: Child Life Zone
description: Developed a vibrant and user-friendly application tailored for the Child Life Zone at Riley Children's Hospital. Prioritized simplicity, colorfulness, and fun in the design to cater to its young users helping to descover what CLZ has to offer. Utilized Adobe XD, Illustrator, HTML, CSS, SCSS, and JavaScript to create user interface and design.

title: College GitHub Repos
description: Check out my college projects on GitHub to see what I’ve learned and built throughout my time in school.
